title: Tangy Saudi Chicken Stew
description: A citrusy, savory stew infused with Saudi spices, ketchup, and a mother sauce base, perfect for a comforting, FODMAP-friendly meal.
instructions: Heat the olive oil and garlic‑infused oil in a heavy pot over medium heat.
Add the chicken thighs and brown on all sides, about 5 minutes per side.
Stir in the paprika, cumin and coriander, cooking for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the tomato paste, ketchup and a pinch of salt, stirring until the chicken is coated.
Pour in the chicken broth, lemon juice and a dash of black pepper, then bring to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and let the stew simmer for 25 minutes, or until the chicken is tender.
Taste and adjust seasoning with additional salt if needed.
Serve the stew hot, spooned over a bed of steamed rice or flatbread.
Garnish with fresh parsley if desired.
